rulings.Case Title: NATHANIEL SCOTT VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 25, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT HAS TAKEN JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE PURCHASE AND ASSUMPTION AGREEMENT BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND THE FDIC. EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452 ("OFFICIAL ACTS"). THAT AGREEMENT PROVIDES THAT THE FDIC, NOT DEFENDANT, ASSUMED THE LIABILITIES FOR THE CLAIMS SUCH AS THOSE ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF IN THIS COMPLAINT RELATED TO WAMU'S ACTS. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (VIOLATION OF THE UCL) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST IDENTIFY THE PARTICULAR STATUTORY SCHEME THAT WAS VIOLATED AND DESCRIBE WITH PARTICULARITY THE FACT SUPPORTING THE STATUTORY ELEMENTS OF THE VIOLATION. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INJUNCTIVE RELIEF) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS WITH THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE SPECIFIC GROUNDS FOR EQUITABLE RELIEF. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT REDMOND VS. GAWKER MEDIA LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Complaint; Points And Authorities In Support; Appendix Of Non California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 25, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANTS ADRIAN COVERT, GAWKER MEDIA LLC, GAWKER SALES LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Motion To Strike The Complaint. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET THE BURDEN OF INVOKING THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S PRODUCT/VENTURE IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC CONCERN. WILBANKS V. WOLK (2004) 121 CAL.APP.4TH 883, 898. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT HE IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON HIS LIBEL ACTION BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S ARTICLE IS PROTECTED OPINION NOT SUBJECT TO A LIBEL ACTION. MOREOVER, USE OF THE WORD "SCAM" DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE MADE AS A FACTUAL ASSERTION BUT AS A HEADING INSINUATING TO READERS THAT THEY SHOULD LOOK FURTHER INTO PLAINTIFF'S TECHNOLOGY BEFORE INVESTING IN IT OR BUYING IT. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. =(302/LMG)
